A few weeks ago Ballinger
openly threw down the gauntlet to
Pinchot in an interview charging

to run the interior department"


Present indications are that the
unusual spectacle of a cabinet
member defending his policies be-
fore a gathering in his own state
will be Witnessed before the con-
gress adjourns.


The annual sessions of the ma
tional irrigation congress have re-
peatedly been marked by an attack
on the secretary of the interior.
Heretofore the attack has always
been made by the monopOlistic in
terests. They hated Secetary

cies prevented development In the
